Privacy Policy

Privacy Policy

Last updated: 27 April 2026

By being a member of the BSSV WhatsApp group, you acknowledge and agree that your participation is subject to the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR – Regulation (EU) 2016/679) and the Austrian Data Protection Act (Datenschutzgesetz – DSG 2018).

The Bangladeshi Students Society in Vienna (BSSV) is committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR – Regulation (EU) 2016/679) and the Austrian Data Protection Act (Datenschutzgesetz – DSG 2018).

1. Data Controller

The data controller responsible for processing your personal data is:

Bangladeshi Students Society in Vienna (BSSV)
Vienna, Austria

2. Scope of This Policy

This Privacy Policy applies to personal data processed in connection with:

  • Membership in the BSSV WhatsApp group(s)
  • Use of this website (bangladeshi-students-society-in-vienna.vercel.app)
  • Any other communication channels operated by BSSV

3. Data Collected When Joining the WhatsApp Group

When you join the BSSV WhatsApp group, the following personal data may be processed:

DataSourcePurpose
Name (as set in WhatsApp)WhatsApp profileMember identification within the community
Phone numberWhatsApp platformRequired to add you to the group
Profile pictureWhatsApp profileDisplayed to other group members
Messages sent in the groupYour activityCommunity communication and information sharing
Status updates (if visible)WhatsApp profileIncidental visibility to group members

Note: BSSV administrators do not collect, store, or export your data outside of WhatsApp. However, all data visible inside the WhatsApp group is accessible to all group members (currently students and community members in Vienna). Group members may be located in Austria or other EU/EEA countries.

4. Legal Basis for Processing

All personal data is processed on the following legal bases under Article 6 GDPR:

  • Art. 6(1)(a) – Consent: By voluntarily joining the BSSV WhatsApp group via the invitation link, you give your explicit, freely given consent to have your profile name, phone number, and profile picture visible to other group members.
  • Art. 6(1)(f) – Legitimate Interests: Maintaining a functional, safe, and informative community group for Bangladeshi students in Vienna constitutes a legitimate interest, provided it does not override your fundamental rights and freedoms.

You may withdraw your consent at any time by leaving the WhatsApp group. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing prior to withdrawal.

5. Purpose of Data Processing

Your personal data is processed exclusively for the following purposes:

  • Community communication – enabling members to connect, ask questions, and share information relevant to student life in Vienna
  • Information sharing – distributing announcements, guides, and updates about student life, visa procedures, accommodation, and events in Vienna
  • Group administration – managing membership, maintaining a respectful environment, and enforcing community guidelines
  • Support and assistance – helping newly arrived Bangladeshi students integrate into life in Vienna

BSSV does not use your data for commercial purposes, advertising, profiling, or automated decision-making.

6. WhatsApp as a Third-Party Platform

The BSSV WhatsApp group is hosted on WhatsApp (operated by Meta Platforms Ireland Limited, 4 Grand Canal Square, Dublin 2, Ireland).

By using WhatsApp, you are also subject to WhatsApp’s own Privacy Policy and Meta’s Data Policy. BSSV has no control over WhatsApp’s data processing. We strongly recommend reviewing:

  • WhatsApp Privacy Policy
  • Meta Privacy Policy

WhatsApp may transfer data outside the EU/EEA. Such transfers are governed by Meta’s standard contractual clauses and adequacy decisions as applicable.

7. Data Sharing and Recipients

BSSV does not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties, except:

  • Other group members – your name, phone number, and profile picture are visible to all members of the WhatsApp group by default (this is inherent to the WhatsApp platform)
  • Legal obligations – if required by Austrian or EU law, or by a competent authority

8. Data Retention

  • BSSV administrators do not maintain a separate database of members’ personal data outside of WhatsApp.
  • Once you leave the group, your phone number is removed and your messages remain in the group chat history visible to remaining members (this is a WhatsApp platform limitation).
  • If you request deletion of any data held by BSSV outside WhatsApp (e.g., from this website’s contact form), we will delete it within 30 days.

9. Website Data

When you visit this website, standard server logs may be processed (IP address, browser type, pages visited, timestamps) by our hosting provider Vercel, Inc. (340 Pine Street, Suite 801, San Francisco, CA 94104, USA). Vercel processes data in accordance with GDPR via standard contractual clauses. See Vercel’s Privacy Policy.

This website does not use tracking cookies, advertising cookies, or third-party analytics.

10. Your Rights Under GDPR and Austrian DSG

As a data subject, you have the following rights under GDPR (Articles 15–22) and the Austrian DSG:

RightDescription
Right of Access (Art. 15 GDPR)Request a copy of the personal data BSSV holds about you
Right to Rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)Request correction of inaccurate personal data
Right to Erasure / “Right to be Forgotten” (Art. 17 GDPR)Request deletion of your personal data where no overriding legitimate purpose exists
Right to Restriction of Processing (Art. 18 GDPR)Request that we limit how we use your data
Right to Data Portability (Art. 20 GDPR)Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
Right to Object (Art. 21 GDPR)Object to processing based on legitimate interests
Right to Withdraw Consent (Art. 7(3) GDPR)Withdraw consent at any time (e.g., by leaving the WhatsApp group)

To exercise any of these rights, contact the BSSV group administrators via the WhatsApp group.

We will respond within 30 days as required by GDPR.

11. Right to Lodge a Complaint

If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Austrian Data Protection Authority (Datenschutzbehörde):

Datenschutzbehörde (DSB)
Barichgasse 40–42
1030 Wien, Austria
Website: www.dsb.gv.at
Email: dsb@dsb.gv.at
Phone: +43 1 52 152-0

You may also contact the data protection authority of your country of residence within the EU.

12. Group Governance and Democratic Structure

BSSV operates as a flat, democratic community. The following governance rules apply to all members and administrators:

12.1 Equal Standing of All Members

No admin holds special authority over any member beyond the technical functions granted by the WhatsApp platform. All group decisions, changes, and actions are made by majority voting only. This reflects the principle of equal treatment under Article 20 of the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights and the democratic values upheld by Austrian civil association law (Vereinsgesetz 2002, BGBl. I Nr. 66/2002).

12.2 Protection Against Arbitrary Removal

Once a member has joined the group and meets all entry requirements, no admin or member has the right to remove them without a majority group vote. Any removal without due process would constitute a violation of the member’s right to fair treatment and community participation. This is grounded in:

  • Article 47 of the EU Charter of Fundamental Rights – right to an effective remedy and fair process
  • Austrian Vereinsgesetz 2002, § 13 – rules on exclusion of members from associations must follow fair procedures

13. Member Consent and Data Obligations

By joining or remaining in the BSSV WhatsApp group, every member explicitly agrees to:

  • EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR – Regulation (EU) 2016/679)
  • Austrian Data Protection Act (Datenschutzgesetz – DSG 2018)
  • WhatsApp Terms of Service and Privacy Policy (Meta Platforms Ireland Limited)

13.1 Prohibition on Sharing Member Data

Sharing any member’s personal data, phone number, profile picture, screenshots, or contact details outside this group without their explicit written consent is strictly prohibited under:

  • Art. 5(1)(b) GDPR – purpose limitation: data shared within the group may only be used for the purposes of that group
  • Art. 5(1)(f) GDPR – integrity and confidentiality: personal data must be protected against unauthorised disclosure
  • Art. 83(4) GDPR – violations may result in administrative fines of up to €10,000,000 or 2% of total worldwide turnover
  • § 63 DSG 2018 – unauthorised processing or disclosure of personal data is a criminal offence under Austrian law, punishable by fines

Violating this rule may expose the responsible member to civil liability under Austrian law (ABGB § 1293 ff. – damages) and potential criminal prosecution under § 118a StGB (unauthorised access to data) and § 51 DSG 2018.

14. Disclaimer for Private Communications

Any private or personal communication between group members — whether inside or outside this group — is considered solely the personal matter of the individuals involved. BSSV and its administrators bear no responsibility for the content, consequences, or disputes arising from such private conversations.

This disclaimer is consistent with:

  • Art. 2(2)(c) GDPR – GDPR does not apply to purely personal or household activities
  • Austrian ABGB (Allgemeines Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch) § 1295 – liability requires a direct causal link; BSSV cannot be held liable for independent personal actions of members

15. Code of Conduct and Prohibited Behaviour

BSSV is committed to maintaining a safe, respectful, and inclusive community. The following behaviour is strictly prohibited:

Prohibited BehaviourApplicable Law
Criminal activity of any kindAustrian Strafgesetzbuch (StGB) – all applicable criminal statutes
Hate speech based on race, religion, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, or disability§ 283 StGB (Verhetzung – incitement to hatred); EU Framework Decision 2008/913/JHA on combating racism and xenophobia
Harassment or bullying of any member§ 107a StGB (Beharrliche Verfolgung – stalking/persistent harassment); § 115 StGB (Beleidigung – defamation/insult)
Offensive or disrespectful behaviourGroup community standards and § 1330 ABGB (protection of honour and reputation)
Sexual harassment§ 218 StGB (sexuelle Belästigung); Gleichbehandlungsgesetz (GlBG) – Austrian Equal Treatment Act
Sharing of illegal content (e.g. piracy, CSAM, incitement)§ 119 StGB, § 207a StGB, and applicable EU directives

Any violation will be acted upon immediately and without prior warning, regardless of the member’s status or length of membership. Depending on severity, action may include:

  1. Immediate removal from the group by admin vote
  2. Report to Austrian authorities (Polizei: 133 / Bundeskriminalamt)
  3. Report to WhatsApp Trust & Safety

16. Purpose of the Group

This group exists exclusively to support and connect Bangladeshi students in Vienna. All members are expected to maintain a respectful, helpful, and welcoming environment for everyone.

This purpose is protected under:

  • Art. 11 EU Charter of Fundamental Rights – freedom of expression and information
  • Art. 12 EU Charter of Fundamental Rights – freedom of assembly and association
  • Austrian Vereinsgesetz 2002 – the right of individuals to form and participate in community associations

Any activity that undermines this purpose — including spam, commercial solicitation, or political campaigning without group consent — is not permitted.

17. Community Guidelines Summary

All BSSV WhatsApp group members are responsible for respecting the privacy and dignity of fellow members:

  • Do not share messages, contact details, or personal information of other group members outside the group without their explicit consent
  • Do not screenshot or forward private conversations of other members
  • Do not add group members to other groups without their consent
  • Do not harass, bully, or disrespect any member in any form
  • Respect all democratic decisions made by group vote

Violation of these guidelines may result in immediate removal from the BSSV community and, where applicable, legal action.

18. Changes to This Privacy Policy & Group Rules

We may update this Privacy Policy and Group Rules from time to time to reflect changes in law or our practices. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page. Significant changes will be announced in the BSSV WhatsApp group.

19. Contact

For any privacy-related questions or requests, please reach out to the BSSV administrators directly via the BSSV WhatsApp group.

BSSV – Bangladeshi Students Society in Vienna
Vienna, Austria

This Privacy Policy and Group Rules are provided in English. In case of any conflict with a translated version, the English version shall prevail.

(BSSV) 🇧🇩 🇦🇹 on Github
Developed by: MD Khasrur Rahman
